channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Esports Nations Cup Chess has 82 Countries Qualified
item.content:encoded:
The regional Chess qualifiers for the Esports Nations Cup have concluded, and now 62 more of the eventual 128 players have been confirmed.

The Esports Nations Cup is a multi-esports event with players representing their home countries while competing for substantial prize money.

Chess counts as an esports through a partnership with chess.com, as 128 players will represent their nations and compete for a share of the $600k prize pool.

Each nation is allowed two players maximum though they could only have one representative receive a direct invite.

Following the qualifiers, at least 82 countries and territories will be represented in Chess at ENC 2026.

Before going any further, consider downloading the free Insider Gaming app for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android.

Esports Nations Cup Chess Qualifiers

In addition to 64 directly invited players, another 56 will qualify through regional online qualifiers, with the final 8 slots given to ENC Wildcards.

As mentioned earlier, 64 players, including Magnus Carlsen, were directly invited via a ranking system which awarded points for recent performances.

Now, an additional 61 players from another 61 countries have qualified through online qualifiers.

  • Related: Magnus Carlsen and other top players skip Grand Chess Tour for $1.5 Esports World Cup

These qualifiers were held in North America, South America, Africa, Western Europe, Eastern Europe, Middle East/India/Central Asia, and East Asia/Southeast Asia/Oceania.

A total of 10 nations that didn’t receive a direct invite managed to secure a single slot through the qualifiers:

  • Honduras
  • Mexico
  • El Salvador
  • Venezuela
  • Morocco
  • Nigeria
  • Algeria
  • Jordan
  • Australia
  • New Zealand

Another slot was given to Amani Alazmi, representing Kuwait, who secured the Host Region Wildcard as the next highest-placing competitor from the host region.

The ENC Wildcard rules intend to create opportunities for underrepresented countries and territories to compete.

There are just two Solidarity Wildcard slots left to be filled.

Should Chess count as an esport? Share your thoughts with us on the Insider Gaming Discord.

In other news, the Esports Nations Cup League of Legends rosters and invites have been confirmed.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: GTA 6 Release Date Has Disappeared and People Are Panicking
item.content:encoded:
Earlier today, Rockstar Games unveiled the official cover art for Grand Theft Auto 6, lifting the lid on an ultimately predictable spot of box art that’ll adorn every copy of the game in just a few months. The team also revealed that, from June 25, pre-orders for the most highly anticipated game ever made will finally go live.

However, in making these changes to the GTA 6 website, Rockstar Games has, for some reason, removed any reference to the game’s release date, which is November 19th. This has, of course, caused some gamers to plunge into a state of panic, thinking it means that a delay is imminent.

Before going any further, consider downloading the free Insider Gaming app for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android.

Where Did The GTA 6 Release Date Go?

When you head to the Rockstar Games website and go to the GTA 6 page, you’ll notice that you’re now welcomed by the snazzy new box art and a bold text strike declaring the pre-order window opening on June 25. The game’s platforms are also mentioned, with them, of course, being the PlayStation 5 and XBOX Series… PC will come later, right?

What you won’t see is a release date:

Before today, that release date was clear for all to see:

Even the social media pages have followed suit:

Now, this could just be Rockstar Games walking the walk and assuming that everyone must know when GTA 6 is coming out, given that it’s set to be the most valuable entertainment launch in the history of mankind (probably).

Or, it could be an oversight that has led Rockstar Games to omit the date because of complacency. Given how s***-hot the team has been developing this title, it’s hard to imagine it’s for this reason.

The pre-order date is now front and centre, and regardless of when GTA 6 is coming out, folks will want to get their copies secured as soon as possible. Rockstar Games knows this and understands that whether the game comes out on November 19 or January 1, 2028, all people want to do right now is pre-order it.

However, that hasn’t stopped some users from taking to social media to panic that the date has suddenly disappeared from Rockstar’s site, despite still being present on any platform that offers a wishlist function.

  • RELATED: New GTA 6 Images Appear Online

Do you think there’s any deeper reason why Rockstar Games has removed the GTA 6 release date from the site? Or do you have a bit more common sense? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Dead by Daylight is free on XBOX, and be sure to subscribe to the Insider Gaming Newsletter.

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Valve Provides Update on Steam Controller Reservations
item.content:encoded:
Valve has provided an update on Steam Controller reservations, offering customers more details on availability and orders.

The Steam Controller remains the only of the three hardware items Valve announced in 2025 to have been released and is available for purchase. The controller has also sold out on various occasions.

Now, a new update provides more details on future reservation windows, so players know when they can get their hands on a Steam Controller if they so desire.

Before going any further, here’s how you can have the cleanest reading experience for this and all of our stories: download the free Insider Gaming App for iOS or Android.  

On Thursday, June 18, Valve announced a new system that will allow players to see when the Steam Controller they order will arrive. There are three potential delivery windows: September 2026, December 2026, or 2027. Should the latter occur, additional information on specific timing will be available at a later date. Perhaps most importantly, Valve says it has no plans to stop production on the controller. Still, current demand is outpacing production, and they want to manage expectations.

“We have no plans to stop making Steam Controller. But as we look at the current demand compared to how many we know we can make by the end of the year, we want to manage expectations as much as we can with regards to when folks can expect to receive their order,” the post on the official Steam blog says.

  • More Steam Hardware News: Steam Machine and Steam Frame Set to Ship in Summer 2026

For anyone hoping to make reservations, Valve says representatives will email customers with an option to buy a controller once their place in the queue comes up. At that point, potential customers will have 72 hours to complete their order before they are removed, giving the next person in line an opportunity.

Insider Gaming will continue to monitor the story and report any updates as more information becomes available. Are you still hoping to get your hands on a Steam Controller? Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server.

For more hardware news, check out the latest on Project Helix from XBOX. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Riot Games Reveals How VCT 2027 Open Circuit Will Work
item.content:encoded:
Riot Games has revealed more of its plans for the 2027 Valorant Champions Tour (VCT) 2027 season.

Earlier this year, Riot revealed it is revamping the VCT format for 2027 with Open Qualifiers and Cups replacing the regular season.

Now, the publisher has revealed more details on how partner teams will work in the open circuits, including how many partners will qualify for Cups.

Plus, Riot has also detailed how teams will be offered financial support to keep the Valorant esports ecosystem healthy.

Before going any further, consider downloading the free Insider Gaming app for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android.

Each Region Will Feature Eight VCT Partners

Initially, it was not clear how partnered teams would work as the VCT moved from franchised leagues to open circuits.

Now, Riot has confirmed that eight partnered teams in each region will start the year with the advantage of an automatic invite to their VCT Kickoff.

VCT Americas, Pacific, and EMEA Kickoff 2027 will feature 12 teams — eight partner teams and four teams from Open Qualifiers.

The top three teams from each Kickoff will then qualify for the first Valorant Masters of the year.

  • Related: Valorant Masters London Fanfest includes esports-themed pub

However, these partnered teams will not qualify for every Cup. Once the season starts, every team will have to earn their spot.

Continued direct invites to further competitions will require strong performances and high finishes.

Meanwhile, VCT CN will be slightly different as partner and visitor teams will receive direct entry into the Kickoff and first two Cups.

VCT CN Kickoff 2027 will be made up of eight partner teams, two visitors, and two teams from Open Qualifiers.

And VCT Pacific will include a Last Chance Qualifier (LCQ), where the top teams from South Asia and Oceania can fight for qualification into Kickoff and Cups.

How the VCT 2027 Season Will Look

The VCT 2027 season will start with Kickoff and a Masters, Cup 1, then a second Masters, then Cup 2, and finally Champions.

Thoughout the year, Open Qualifiers will feed into these tournaments as will the Wild Card pathway for VCT Pacific.

This format will lead to teams, even partners, being promoted and relegated to different starting points for later events.

Continued progression will be rewarded while more opportunities will be given to the wider competitive Valorant ecosystem.

Teams that place outside the top positions at a Cup or Masters will move down into Open Playoffs and have to fight their way back up.

New teams with no previous results, or teams that perform poorly at the previous Opens will have to start back at the beginning of the next Open Qualifiers.

Riot also specified that invites and progress for teams will remain for Open Qualifier teams, as long as they keep three of their five players.

This means new and content-creator-led teams can compete throughout the year, as long as they don’t make too many changes.

Financial Support for Non-Partner Teams

Riot has promised to offer “predictable financial support” for open teams that qualify for major events.

Open teams that perform well will receive fixed payments based on event qualification:

  • Qualify for Kickoff or a Cup: $100,000
  • Qualify for Masters: $200,000
  • Qualify for Champions: $400,000

Open teams will also be eligible for prize pool earnings, plus there will also be additional incentives.

One example given is if a non-partner team qualifies for the Game Changers Championship, that team will receive an additional $100,000 incentive.

What do you make of these changes for Riot? Share your reactions with us on the Insider Gaming Discord.

In other news, Fnatic’s CEO has confirmed the organisation “isn’t going anywhere” despite takeover bids.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/riot-games-reveals-how-vct-2027-open-circuit-will-work/

item.title: Esports World Cup: Level Up Documentary Season Two Confirmed
item.content:encoded:
Season Two of the documentary series ‘Esports World Cup: Level Up’ has been confirmed.

The third iteration of the Esports World Cup is set to begin on July 6, 2026, with a $75m combined prize pool.

Meanwhile, the new five-episode docuseries looking behind the scenes at last year’s $70m event will release on June 26 on Prime Video.

The series was directed by Emmy-winning filmmaker R.J. Cutler, who also worked on the Billie Eilish: The World’s A Little Blurry documentary.

While primarily based in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia, where last year’s event took place, the series also visits the UK, US, and Indonesia to the homes of highlighted players including Fnatic’s Jake ‘Boaster’ Howlett.

Before going any further, consider downloading the free Insider Gaming app for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android.

Esports World Cup Level Up Season Two Storylines

The five episodes of season two of EWC Level Up will dive into the stories of some of the tournament’s biggest names and most interesting backstories.

One such player is Magnus Carlsen, considered one of the greatest Chess players ever, who won the event last year while representing Team Liquid.

As mentioned earlier, Fnatic Valorant star Boaster will also be featured in the series, starring his journey from aspiring actor to an esports great.

There will also be a look at Zeng ‘Xiao Hai’ Zhuojun, who won the first-ever Street Fighter EWC title and then looked to balance defending his title with his family life after becoming a father.

  • Related: Esports World Cup 2026 relocated to Paris

Additionally, season two of Level Up will star Team Vitality’s Mobile Legends: Bang Bang pro Vivi ‘Vivian’ Indrawaty as she looked to overcome childhood trauma while competing in the only women’s tournament at the event.

The documentary will also follow the all-Mongolian roster of The Mongolz, who surprised everyone when they won the Counter-Strike 2 title at EWC last year.

Plus, there will also be an appearance from Team Falcons’ Call of Duty: Warzone star Kasimili ‘Soka’ Tongamoa, who battles against former teammates while dealing with a turbulent home life.

Once it releases, share your reactions to the documentary with us on the Insider Gaming Discord.

Additionally, see a week-by-week breakdown of the Esports World Cup 2026 schedule.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/esports-world-cup-level-up-documentary-season-two-confirmed/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: PlayStation CEO: ‘A Game Console is Necessary For Playing Games’
item.content:encoded:
In a recent interview, Hideaki Nishino, the CEO of PlayStation, reaffirmed his potentially inflammatory belief about the traditional concept of a games console. In his words, the existence of a games console is necessary for playing games, but the embodiment of a games console might be somewhat open to interpretation.

I say that because Nishino then went on to praise the PlayStation Portal, saying how it allowed gamers to enjoy titles from other areas and not just from the living room, in front of a television. Do you agree with Nishino’s belief?

Before going any further, consider downloading the free Insider Gaming app for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android.

Is a Console a Must-Have For Today’s Gamers?

Up above, I said that Nishino’s belief might seem inflammatory to some. It’s a classic ‘hot take’, if you will. In a world where PC gaming is becoming increasingly popular and mobile gaming is the most valuable and widespread vertical in the business, it might seem bold for a gaming CEO to suggest that you need a console to enjoy today’s top titles.

Of course, he’d be in hot water if he said that a console isn’t exactly needed to enjoy games. This isn’t XBOX, right?

Here’s the full quote, which surfaced recently in a special interview with Famitsu, a Japanese outlet:

My belief that a game console is necessary for playing games remains unchanged. I think we can create something interesting by utilizing technologies that can be used in various forms and locations to develop new game console experiences.

For example, while PlayStation is strongly associated with playing on a TV in the living room, we developed the PlayStation Portal to enable comfortable gameplay in other locations as well. We want to constantly take on new challenges so that we can provide gaming experiences that fit diverse lifestyles.

In one paragraph, Nishino effectively put console gaming, the worst-performing slice of the wider business, on a pedestal, praised the PlayStation Portal, a divisive device at best, and suggested that other, similar innovations might be in store.

He mentions how PlayStation wants to take on new challenges to provide gaming experiences that fit diverse lifestyles. Perhaps there’s another handheld avenue in Sony’s future? I’d consider buying a PlayStation Vita 2, that’s for sure.

  • RELATED: PS Plus Game Catalog for June 2026

Nishino, who accepted a dual role as President and CEO of Sony Interactive Entertainment last year, also addressed the recent drama around PlayStation pulling first-party games away from PC platforms. This was a huge point of debate last month, when Hermen Hulst, another PlayStation CEO, confirmed the strategy.

Nishino said:

If releasing a title on PC would maximize the gaming experience, we’ll continue to consider that option. Our current main policy is that, for single-player games developed in-house, we will further refine the value of the gaming experience that PlayStation can offer.

At the same time, we believe it is important for live-service games to reach a wider audience through online multiplayer, so we continue to view releases on both PS5 and PC as the standard.

What do you want to see from the future of PlayStation when it comes to both hardware and software? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Dead by Daylight is free on XBOX, and be sure to subscribe to the Insider Gaming Newsletter.

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

item.title: ENC: European League of Legends Online Qualifier Brackets
item.content:encoded:
The Esports Nations Cup (ENC) online League of Legends qualifier brackets for both EU East and EU West have been revealed.

The Esports Foundation confirmed the 16 invited LoL ENC teams and rosters that topped the charts for the highest performance scores.

Now, another 14 slots need to be decided by online regional qualifiers, with two expected to qualify from both EU qualifiers.

Here is everything you need to know about the ENC LoL online qualifiers for European teams.

Before going any further, consider downloading the free Insider Gaming app for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android.

When Are the ENC LoL European Qualifiers?

Both the Europe East and Europe West online qualifiers begin on Friday June 19.

They will also both end on June 21 with two teams from each (four in total) qualifying for the final tournament.

As for the ENC qualifier format, all matches will be best-of-ones except for the Lower Bracket and Upper Bracket finals, which will be best-of-threes.

  • Related: Esports Nations Cup Rocket League rosters

ENC LoL Europe East Qualifier Rosters

  • Albania: Diobello / Kr0n / Alvanai / Ralf / iLevi
  • Bulgaria: Archfiend / Spooky / FrostSize / Vzz / Infoneral
  • Croatia: Sacre / Mafro / Perkz / Jopa / ThomasFate
  • Estonia: Ruf / yOFT / Klaabu / Jabuticaba / Richu
  • Hungary: Vizicsacsi / bluerzor / Robenong / Endless / Passzi
  • Israel: Music / Chelouche / Tissue / Shogo / Balagan
  • Kosovo: Supreme light / Desserte / Paradox / al1nyyy / Arsick
  • Latvia: Onyoz / Qualzy / Hjustons / Afriibi / Mango
  • Lithuania: Eckas / Lyncas / Toffe / Yashiro / Parein
  • Moldova: Kpr / Owlonsky / Babuleh / LeX / Hokage
  • Montenegro: Talja / Pegaz / Nafkelah / Lixo / Sheka
  • North Macedonia: Danki / Fallen / Pesho / Axelent / Nano
  • Romania: Shelfmade / Frost / Ronaldo / Yakkey / whiteinn
  • Serbia: APP / Stefan / VeRu / Shy Carry / Panj
  • Slovakia: Stein / zeen / SeMike / Raining / AwerpiS
  • Slovenia: Kektz / Nighthawk / Foxie / Sentio / KroMAX
  • Ukraine: Maynter / Maul / Fogzy / Jamie / Efias

ENC LoL Europe East Qualifier Bracket and Format

The online qualifiers will feature a double-elimination bracket, lose in the Lower Bracket and you’re out.

As mentioned prior, all matches will be best-of-ones except for the two finals, which will be best-of-threes to decide who qualifies for this year’s Esports Nations Cup.

In Europe East, only two teams will play in the opening round having been ranked lower than the other teams.

Opening Matches

  • Upper Bracket Round 1
    • Moldova vs Estonia
  • Upper Bracket Round 2
    • Serbia vs Moldova/Estonia
    • Romania vs Latvia
    • Ukraine vs Slovenia
    • Lithuania vs Kosovo
    • Croatia vs Bulgaria
    • Albania vs North Macedonia
    • Hungary vs Israel
    • Slovakia vs Montenegro

ENC LoL Europe West Rosters

  • Austria: Vertigo / Konkai / UniqueCORN / DizL / seaz
  • Belgium: Bwipo / Isma / Nisqy / Évangelyne / Targamas
  • Cyprus: Totyz / Limbo / Ivok / Zebron / SP1Nx
  • Finland: Nille / dibu / Simpli / Lari / Sammey
  • Germany: Irrelevant / Habubu / Reeker / Keduii / Tockimo
  • Great Britain: Shikari / White / BZ / Deadly / kaSing
  • Iceland: Baunadós / Icelandic Hero / Sigginn / Meoki / Kristut
  • Ireland: Allorim / Hybradge / BB Muffin / jinjo / Markgaffo
  • Italy: Gabbo / Goliah / Vigil / Ryuk / Click
  • Malta: Gruntless / Jar / Drakur / Hido / Awaken
  • Netherlands: Dan Dan / Markoon / Febiven / Furuy / Seal
  • Norway: Smurfegutten / DenSygeKamel69 / LIDER / Nomi / Nash
  • Portugal: PapiTeero / Time / Baca / FlickeR / Obstinatus
  • Switzerland: Pride / MoTo / Poro / Yen / Lucky

ENC LoL Europe West Qualifier Bracket and Format

While Europe West has the same format as the East qualifier, the bracket is slightly different.

Germany and Italy were higher ranked than the other teams, so received a bye into Upper Bracket Round 2.

Meanwhile, the rest of the teams will play in Upper Bracket Round 1.

Opening Matches

  • Ireland vs Norway
    • Winner faces Germany
  • Portugal vs Iceland
  • Belgium vs Malta
  • Great Britain vs Austria
    • Winner faces Italy
  • Netherlands vs Cyprus
  • Finland vs Switzerland

Which four teams do you expect to qualify? Let us now on the Insider Gaming Discord.

In other news, the LCS Spring Split 2026 recorded its lowest-ever viewership despite Jynxzi co-streaming the final.
